content_markdown:
The graph shows the variation with pressure $p$ of the volume $V$ of a sample of gas.

The work done during the change of state from X to Y is

diagram_metadata.description: A graph of Volume V (y-axis) against Pressure p (x-axis). A right-angled triangle is formed by points X, Y, and Z. Point X is at (p1, V1), point Y is at (p1, V2), and point Z is at (p2, V1). The process from X to Y is a vertical line at constant pressure p1.

vibe_explanation: Work done by a gas is defined as $W = \int p \, dV$. In the process from X to Y, the pressure is constant at $p_1$ while the volume changes from $V_1$ to $V_2$. Therefore, the work done is the pressure multiplied by the change in volume: $W = p_1(V_2 - V_1)$. Note that on a $V$-$p$ graph (as opposed to the standard $p$-$V$ graph), the work done is the area under the curve relative to the volume axis.
